title: phanera kockiana
intro: Phanera kockiana, also known as the Indian coral tree, is a deciduous tree species native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its vibrant flowers and striking red trunk. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots, which are said to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phanera kockiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant are often made into decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its therapeutic properties.
